Alright, welcome back. In this lesson, we are going to talk about a very important distinction, one between sales scripts, which you may be familiar with, you may even be using, and sales frameworks, which is what the CTC enrollment process is built off of. So we're going to break down the difference between a script and a framework, and why I believe, and in my experience, frameworks lend themselves far greater to the reality of what is actually happening on an enrollment conversation. Cool. So structure versus stifling rigidity. So expecting that a sale must happen exactly between minutes 55 to 65 after you've gone through the seven core steps of your script is not that much different than saying you must fall in love by dessert on our fifth date. So there's a lot of parallels to be made here. Typically marketing loves to go crazy between the dating and the sales metaphor. But the reason for that is it kind of does work, right? And when we talk about rigid scripts on a sales call, where we are trying to coach someone towards a life changing reality and a decision, much like a relationship, kind of doesn't make sense to go in such a rigid linear way, right? So on a sales call, the typical script will be your agenda setting, and then you'll talk about the pain and the problem and all the ways that manifest in their life and what might happen if they don't get this solved. And then you'll talk about the desire and the goal and all the outcomes they want and what life will look like there. And then you will position yourself in your program as the bridge between this shitty reality and the reality they want here, then you'll make the offer, then you'll counter objections, and you'll go for the close, right? It's kind of like developing a relationship, another life changing decision saying on date one, we're going to have coffee, we're going to make small talk, I'm going to ask you where you were from, where you went to school, why you're doing what you're doing in life. Then day two, we're going to talk about all your failed relationships, all the things you really didn't like about it, all the things that didn't feel good. Then we're going to talk about what you want in a partner, what is your checklist, what is your dream marriage, what is your dream reality. Then on date four, we're going to talk about how you're, how I'm all those things, right? And how you can trust me to be this bridge between all the shittiness of your past and all the places you want to go. And on date five, I'm going to propose that we fall in love and on date six, I'm going to counter all the objections that come up and all your resistance around me. And on date seven, man, you need to be convinced that I'm the one and I'm going to pressure you at all costs into believing that. So of course, this is a bit of an exaggeration. But hopefully the point is clear, right? That when we're coaching life-changing decisions, expecting it to progress in such a linear, systematic, step-by-step rigid way, just kind of runs counter to the actual nature in which these things, business relationships or romantic relationships actually develop. So remember, the states and precursors that lead to the buying decision are nonlinear, much like falling in love, the inner knowingness of rightness, for example, can click and occur in the first 30 seconds of being in your presence, or it could take longer for someone to be available and receptive and attuned to it. So you can't have a rigid linear process and expect a quantum result. They're just simply misaligned. Yet, much like a romantic relationship, you do need to have some degree of structure and direction to provide enough safety and coherence for that change in transformation to actually occur. Otherwise, you descend into something else you don't want, which is chaos and confusion, noncoherence. So scripts tend to be linear and investigative, almost like you're a journalist recording their history, and there's not much actual availability to these magical aha moments and insights and transformation, whereas frameworks are flexible, more integrative, and more lending themselves towards these intangible layers of experience that actually favor the sale. So what you'll typically get on a linear script or an investigative sales call is they'll break up the goal, desire and pleasure with the pain, problem and struggle. It's a rigid structure, it's a rigid script. The salesperson will typically be the one setting the pace of the call. Leadership comes from controlling the frame, right, maintaining the frame and structure of the call, even when it's inorganic to the flow of the conversation. It tends to be focused, driven forward in its approach. And the offer, which is presented, tends to be future focus. These are all the things that you'll experience in the future, and I'm solving this problem for you through this solution. And it tends to be fear-based, both for the marketer, who is deeply insecure of losing the sale, and for the prospect, where the reason to buy is to escape the fear of what could go wrong versus step into the rightness of the new opportunity before them, which makes it manipulation-based and just simply high pressure, right, and time bound. The sale must happen in this 55-minute time frame, even though it is not a linear based decision, but something that actually occurs based on all these states, all these milestones that are a little less linear in of themselves. Whereas quantum frameworks, and integrative enrollment calls, which we're going to be exploring, recognize that pain, pleasure, problem, outcome, all these things are actually inseparable, right? And we masterfully weave them together while unpacking the totality of our prospects experience around the goal and the problem. And this is what we mean by gestalt, which is going to be the whole topic of the next module. And to do this, to unpack it, we are more flexible in our structure and our framework, and our leadership doesn't come from controlling the frame at all costs, but from it being attuned to the conversation we're having, to being empathetic, to being dynamic in our ability to adjust the pace of the call, where we're taking things, to not be beholden to a structure that may not make sense, but actually change the pace, change the conversation in accordance with our prospects process that we're in constant attunement to. The offer itself isn't only about what will happen in the future. It's also redeeming and inclusive of the past, it's resolution driven, it's resolving all their misconceptions, all their pain, all their data backed doubt about the past. So the offer is inclusive of the past, and moves them towards the future simultaneously. It's courage-based, both for the marketer and the prospect, not fear-based and it's transformation-based. Once again, we are making this sale, this empowered relationship a natural emergent of all these things that have happened to gain trust, to gain a sense of power, possibility, and the safety to pursue it. And the sale will happen when it's ready to happen, much like a romantic relationship. There will be the proper follow-up, but we're not holding anyone accountable to a decision within 55 minutes or on date seven, or for a more X-rated version, orgasming within 20 minutes or else something's wrong here. We are letting it occur when it is naturally ready to emerge through our leadership and through our coaching. Cool, so scripts are typically more used as training wheels until you're ready to use more powerful and adaptive tools. If you are watching this, you are ready to learn and use those tools. In fact, it's probably been a bit of a crutch trying to rely on these scripts that feel rigid and stifling of who you actually are. But ultimately, the best sales process is the one you feel most aligned, alive, and empowered by. So let's consider for a moment that you don't have a problem with sales and the idea of rejection. You have a problem with the dissonance that what you're doing on sales calls really isn't of service. So your energy, your presence on the call and your confidence is going to be 80% of the work, how you show up and how you feel entering that ecosystem, that container is 80% of the work. The remaining 20% of leadership and coaching someone through an empowered decision is fueled by your energy. It's fueled by that 80%. And scripts tend to be stifling and in disservice of that critical 80%. So instead, the CTC is a framework. It's a set of states that you're going to be coaching and milestones that need to be achieved for the yes to be a natural emergent by the end of it. The shift from prospect to client once again, is a quantum leap that they effortlessly make when enough of these states and thresholds have been crossed after you've led them through enough of these transformations and thresholds that leap into an effortless yes is going to come more natural. So a big part of this program is going to be unpacking what these states are, what these milestones are. And ultimately, at a very high level, the inner anatomy of an empowered partner, an empowered buyer, an empowered client, not one who complied is one who has felt and achieved a degree of resonance around you, your brand, your offer, your ability to assess what's gone wrong in the past. It's going to be about relevance connecting their unique needs, their unique capacities and abilities with your offer and the things are going to be doing throughout your program, a sense of resourcefulness, how resourceful do they feel in tapping into new possibilities and their ability to get resourceful around what's needed to achieve it this time around. Of course, achievability comes online once we feel resourceful. Accountability is something we're coaching feeling accountable to a decision, right to a yes or to a no, making either safe, but holding people accountable for their lives for their decisions. And also honoring our boundaries, right when we decide to invest ourselves in talking with someone and coaching them through this process, holding someone to a standard of accountability is no different than holding a set of boundaries around our energy around our time around our service as well. So accountability is a big part of it. Then finally actualization coaching people to a point where they feel like they can actualize all this potentiality that they've already explored with you. And of course, meeting the safety to experience all these new states, right, and the power and possibility to actually actualize it. So this is the crux of the CTC enrollment call. This is what we're going to be coaching and facilitating on our enrollment calls. In our automated intimacy model, we also talked about the ideas of coaching mastery, magnetism and momentum, which are also critical to the enrollment process. This stuff is actually facilitated in the time between booking and getting on the call. So if you do want to learn how to coach these elements, you can learn more about that in automated intimacy or in application funnel amplifiers specific to sales calls. But that's it. This is what we are coaching at a glance. The rest of the program is going to be about unpacking all this and the tools and skills and coaching techniques that actually help us uncover what's needed to make our prospects, make our pre customers feel resonance, relevance, resourceful, a sense of achievability, a sense of accountability to a decision and a sense of actualizing all these things that now became possible for them. So take a breather and I will meet you in the next lesson.
